title: An A/B Test That Lies to You
description: Your A/B numbers tell two different stories depending on how you slice them. Walk through Simpson's paradox with a data lead.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: The overall A/B test says variant B wins. Per region, variant A wins everywhere. What's going on?

templates.text: Classic Simpson's paradox. Group trends can flip when you aggregate.
templates.hint_zh: 經典 Simpson 悖論。分組的趨勢匯總後可能反轉。

templates.text: Usually a confounder. Different regions have very different sample sizes or base rates.
templates.hint_zh: 通常是干擾變數。各區的樣本量或基礎率差很多。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We had eighty percent of variant B users in our highest-converting region.

templates.text: There it is. The aggregate is just measuring the region mix, not the variant.
templates.hint_zh: 問題就在這。匯總其實在量區域組成,不是變體效果。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So which result do we trust?

templates.text: Per-region. The aggregate is misleading until we control for the imbalance.
templates.hint_zh: 用分區結果。匯總在調平之前都會誤導人。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: How do we report this without confusing leadership?

templates.text: Show both views, label the confounder, and recommend re-randomizing the next test.
templates.hint_zh: 兩個視角都呈現,標出干擾變數,建議下次重新隨機分配。

templates.text: Thanks. Easy mistake to ship if we just trusted the headline.
templates.hint_zh: 謝。要是我們只信頭條數字,這錯誤很容易就出貨了。
